does: garak is a comprehensive framework for security probing of large language models that systematically discovers and identifies vulnerabilities through structured adversarial testing. The framework automates the process of red-teaming by generating diverse adversarial inputs across multiple threat categories and evaluating model responses to reveal weaknesses. Rather than applying one-size-fits-all security evaluations, garak enables discovery-oriented exploration of how LLMs respond to attacks, documenting specific vulnerabilities and failure modes in target models or dialog systems. The framework structures security assessment around the observation that LLM vulnerabilities are context-dependent and constantly evolving as models are updated and deployed in new applications.
connects: This work responds to the challenge of scaling security evaluation as LLMs are rapidly deployed at scale across numerous applications. It builds on prior red-teaming and adversarial evaluation practices from machine learning security, extending these approaches specifically for the unpredictable and diverse threat landscape of language models. garak addresses the limitation of static guardrails and one-fits-all security solutions by drawing on research in adversarial robustness, safety evaluation, and model behavior characterization. The framework consolidates emerging practices in LLM security testing into a unified tool, recognizing that previous approaches treating security as a fixed property rather than a context-dependent characteristic are insufficient for deployed systems.
enables: garak establishes infrastructure for continuous and scalable vulnerability discovery in deployed LLMs, enabling organizations to systematically assess model weaknesses before and after deployment. The framework supports the development of context-specific defenses by documenting failure modes across diverse threat categories and use cases. It provides a foundation for security benchmarking and comparative evaluation of different models and safety interventions. By automating adversarial probing, garak facilitates feedback loops between security researchers and model developers, supporting iterative improvement of guardrails and safety measures. The work points toward holistic LLM security practices that treat vulnerability discovery as an ongoing process rather than a one-time evaluation.
